findings.description: Multi-column form layout and side-by-side elements are flattened into a single column.
findings.evidence: On PDF page 2, the top section uses a distinct two-column layout (contact fields on the left, option boxes on the right), side-by-side fields (e.g., City, State, Zip Code), and a three-column footer. The HTML renders all of these sections sequentially in a single vertical column.

findings.description: Logo graphic is missing from the form header.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 features an NCDOR logo graphic in the top-left corner. The HTML conversion omits this image, rendering only the text 'NCDOR' at the beginning of the form content.

findings.description: Multiple h1 tags are used in the document.
findings.evidence:
The HTML contains '

Instructions For Handwritten Forms

' and later '

NCDOR

'. Only one h1 should be used as the main document title.
